              <mo>;</mo>

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.description: The dependent variable representing the wave displacement field
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the dependent variable in the wave equation and appears multiple times in the weak formulation.

concepts_list.name: w
concepts_list.description: Test function used in the weak formulation of the wave equation
concepts_list.justification: Appears repeatedly as a test function in integrals and weak form equations, with the same notation across different equations.

concepts_list.name: rho
concepts_list.description: Mass per unit volume of the medium
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the positive mass per unit volume in the linear wave equation context.

concepts_list.name: T
concepts_list.description: Tensile pre-stress load per unit surface area
concepts_list.justification: Described as a positive tensile pre-stress load per unit surface area in the physical description of the wave equation.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.description: The main spatial domain partitioned into subdomains
concepts_list.justification: Referred to as the d-dimensional spatial domain and partitioned into subdomains Ω_i; appears in integrals and boundary conditions.

concepts_list.name: Gamma
concepts_list.description: The collection of interfaces between subdomains
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the collection of interfaces between subdomains Ω_i, and appears in transmission conditions and integrals over boundaries.

concepts_list.name: t
concepts_list.justification: Used as the independent variable in time derivatives and appears in initial conditions and time-dependent formulations.

concepts_list.name: H_0_1_Omega
concepts_list.description: Sobolev space of functions with zero trace on the boundary of Ω
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as H^1_0(Ω) and referenced in the weak formulation as the space where the solution and test functions reside.

concepts_list.name: H_1
concepts_list.description: Space of functions in H^1(Ω) over time with zero trace
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the collection of H^1_0(Ω)-valued functions in time, appearing in the weak formulation as the space of solutions.

concepts_list.name: H_minus1
concepts_list.description: Dual space of H^1_0(Ω) used in duality pairing
concepts_list.justification: Appears as the dual space of H^1_0(Ω) in the weak form, denoted as H^{-1}(Ω), in the duality pairing notation.

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Differential element of integration over the spatial domain Ω
concepts_list.justification: Used in integrals over Ω and Γ, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Γ, indicating it is the integration variable over the domain.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Differential element of integration over the interface domain Γ
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over Γ, such as ∫_Γ, indicating it is the integration variable over the interface boundary.

concepts_list.name: g
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary condition value on the Neumann part of the boundary
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the boundary value in the Neumann condition equation (4b), appearing in the weak form as a boundary term.

concepts_list.name: cal_T
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the temporal domain T = [0, t_end] and used in the formulation of the weak solution over time.

new_concept_info.description:
The dependent variable representing the wave displacement field
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as the dependent variable in the wave equation and appears multiple times in the weak formulation.

Test function used in the weak formulation of the wave equation
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears repeatedly as a test function in integrals and weak form equations, with the same notation across different equations.

Mass per unit volume of the medium
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the positive mass per unit volume in the linear wave equation context.

Tensile pre-stress load per unit surface area
JUSTIFICATION:
Described as a positive tensile pre-stress load per unit surface area in the physical description of the wave equation.

The main spatial domain partitioned into subdomains
JUSTIFICATION:
Referred to as the d-dimensional spatial domain and partitioned into subdomains Ω_i; appears in integrals and boundary conditions.

The collection of interfaces between subdomains
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the collection of interfaces between subdomains Ω_i, and appears in transmission conditions and integrals over boundaries.

Temporal variable representing time
JUSTIFICATION:
Used as the independent variable in time derivatives and appears in initial conditions and time-dependent formulations.

Sobolev space of functions with zero trace on the boundary of Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as H^1_0(Ω) and referenced in the weak formulation as the space where the solution and test functions reside.

Space of functions in H^1(Ω) over time with zero trace
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the collection of H^1_0(Ω)-valued functions in time, appearing in the weak formulation as the space of solutions.

Dual space of H^1_0(Ω) used in duality pairing
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears as the dual space of H^1_0(Ω) in the weak form, denoted as H^{-1}(Ω), in the duality pairing notation.

Differential element of integration over the spatial domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
Used in integrals over Ω and Γ, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Γ, indicating it is the integration variable over the domain.

Differential element of integration over the interface domain Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
Appears in integrals over Γ, such as ∫_Γ, indicating it is the integration variable over the interface boundary.

Neumann boundary condition value on the Neumann part of the boundary
JUSTIFICATION:
Defined as the boundary value in the Neumann condition equation (4b), appearing in the weak form as a boundary term.

Temporal domain denoted as [0, t_end]
JUSTIFICATION:
Explicitly defined as the temporal domain T = [0, t_end] and used in the formulation of the weak solution over time.
